scenario: Helios Airline is planning a Camunda 8.8 booking platform that will move 8500 messages per day between Zeebe and three downstream systems for a regional carrier. The lead architect wants to align the team on the three integration technologies the best-practices guide highlights as most common when teams write custom glue code.
question: Which technology examples does the best-practices guide highlight as the most common integration targets for custom glue code?

options.text: GraphQL, MQTT, and Apache Pulsar are the three integration technologies highlighted with code samples in the technology examples section of the best-practices guide
Apache Camel, Mulesoft, and IBM MQ are the three integration technologies highlighted with code samples in the technology examples section of the best-practices guide
WebSockets, gRPC streaming, and Apache Cassandra are the three integration technologies highlighted with code samples in the technology examples section of the best-practices guide

REST endpoints, messaging such as AMQP and RabbitMQ, and Apache Kafka are the three integration technologies highlighted with code samples in the technology examples section of the best-practices guide

a.text: Incorrect. GraphQL, MQTT, and Apache Pulsar are not the examples on the page; the documented examples are REST, messaging (AMQP/RabbitMQ), and Kafka.

b.text: Incorrect. Apache Camel, Mulesoft, and IBM MQ are not the examples on the page; the documented examples are REST, messaging, and Kafka.

c.text: Incorrect. WebSockets, gRPC streaming, and Cassandra are not the examples on the page; the documented examples are REST, messaging, and Kafka.

d.text: Correct. The technology examples section calls out REST endpoints, messaging (often AMQP via RabbitMQ), and Apache Kafka topics, and links to the Ticket Booking and Flowing Retail examples for each.

explanation: The technology examples section explicitly says most projects ask for REST, messaging, or Kafka. It links to a Java/Spring Boot REST endpoint that starts process instances, a RabbitMQ/AMQP example that correlates inbound messages with running instances, and a Kafka example using Spring Cloud Streams to subscribe to topics and emit records from service tasks.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
